title: DispatchSpec
description: Director-to-Specialist dispatch packet. Validated before any sub-agent launch. Schema version 2: adds threshing_input_paths for P-VS-06 + extends role enum with Quaker pipeline roles. Backward-compatible with v1 dispatches via schemas/migrations/v1_to_v2.py.

dispatch_altitude.description: Altitude of work — META (org-shaping), SPEC (design), IMPL (build), INFRA (lab tooling), NIT (small fix).

role.description: Role name from agents/ directory or general-purpose if no fit. v2 adds Quaker pipeline roles: 'threshing_pass' (P-VS-06; surfaces disagreement, never decides), 'clearness_phase1' (P-VS-07 phase 1; open queries), 'clearness_phase2' (P-VS-07 phase 2; verdict with phase-1 queries as context). NOT in enum because role registry is dynamic; KNOWN_ROLES in core/subagent.py is the runtime check.

output_path.description: Where the agent must write its full output. Distinct paths per parallel agent of same role (P-cycle suffix).

caveats_embedded.description: Pre-registered caveats from prior cycles relevant to this dispatch.

forbidden_actions.description: Things this agent must not do (e.g., 'don't touch pi_notes.md').

process_hygiene.description: Reminder of process rules relevant to this dispatch (>20 chars).

confidence_required.description: If true, agent must return calibrated confidence + reasoning ≥80 chars.

depends_on.description: Roles or sub-task IDs this dispatch depends on.

falsifier_text.description: Outside-observer-checkable falsifier (>30 chars). What would make this dispatch wrong?

verification_command.description: Optional shell command run by Spawn AFTER the agent loop completes. Exit code 0 = pass; nonzero = fail. Output is captured into ResultPacket.telemetry.verification. The agent's self-reported verdict is overridden when this disagrees: pass+claimed_fail → BUILD_PASS, fail+claimed_pass → BUILD_FAIL. If the agent crashed without writing the ResultPacket but verification passes, Spawn synthesizes a clean BUILD_PASS instead of returning OTHER/result_packet_missing.

threshing_input_paths.description: P-VS-06: array of disagreeing-position output paths to surface in threshing pass. When present, this dispatch IS a threshing pass — the agent reads these (typically conflicting) outputs and surfaces the substance of the disagreement WITHOUT rendering a verdict. ResultPacket.verdict MUST be SCOPE_STOP. Source: BYM Quaker faith & practice §12.26 (https://qfp.quaker.org.uk/passage/12-26/), Sheeran 1983 ch. 3.

clearness_phase1_output_path.description: P-VS-07 phase 2 only: path to phase-1 ClearnessQuery array output. Phase 2 dispatch reads this as context. If present, this dispatch is phase 2; otherwise it's a non-clearness verdict.
